feed.subtitle: A hobby blog covering Warhammer, painting, conversions, and grimdark projects.

entry.title: The Dreadclops
entry.content:
“It walks like a man, until it doesn’t m’lord. All of a sudden it moves like the devil itself, so fast it were m’lord, if I hadn’t seen what it done to poor Harry I wouldn’t ave believed it. No m’lord, I don’t suppose it did see me watch, or if it did it didn’t seem to care none. If I may m’lord, I don’t claim to be an expert about such matters, I leave that to gentlemen like yerself m’lord, but I will venture that when you rip it’s helmet off, you’re not going to be looking at anything human.

- Survivor Testimony

On the eve of the first night, I lay awake thinking of the tales that we’d been told on the way in. The locals were clearly spooked by whatever this thing was, initially nobody wanted to talk about it, well, not until we applied the correct forms of pressure. Said pressure in this case turned out to be a rather pitiful amount of Crown Gelt, and before we knew it we had more data than we could sift through. The Interrogator, Phelps, had put me in charge of sorting through everything that the small army of Savants would be sending over and organising it by priority of investigation. That was all for the next day.

As I lay on the cot I thought I heard a clanging sound coming from the street. It was late, way past curfew, so nobody other than the local Arbites should be out and about. I roused as the clanging continued, and headed to the small window that looked out onto the tenement row below. It was dark, and there was a thick layer of the smog that constantly choked the precinct present as always, I squinted hard.

The Dreadclops was one of the first miniatures I built from scratch. Him and his little minion “Frankle” were super fun to build, the idea of using the Spindle Drone head had been floating around my brain, and when I picked up some really cheap Stormcast minis there was only one real option for what was going to happen next. Slap in some extra bits from various Necromunda kits and they came together really nicely.

entry.title: Too Many Eyes
entry.content:
How many eyes is too many eyes?

However many eyes the thing had.

They blinked, they rolled, they winked.

Some bled, some wept, some blind.

Too many eyes.

Unfocused, wandering, wondering.

Too many eyes.

Focused now, many eyes are one eye.

They see me.

Too many eyes see through me.

They do not wander.

I dare not move or speak.

It speaks from a mouth that I cannot see.

It tells me that it sees me.

I cannot move or speak.

Something silver, something sharp, something quick.

Now I see, with too many eyes.

entry.title: Rest in Peace: John Blanche
entry.content:
The Bell of Lost Souls will now ring 40,000 times.

There are no words to truly convey the impact that John Blanche had on the world of Warhammer and miniature painting. His art, commonly referred to by the community as “Blanchitsu”, pioneered the “grim dark” style that defined the early editions of Warhammer 40,000, and would be the pillar upon which everything else was built. It’s a reductive method of painting that uses a fairly sparse colour pallete, mostly focusing on earthy tones such as reds, oranges, and browns, but blended in a way that instantly tells a story. It’s an instantly recognisable style, especially for those of us who have been around the hobby for a long time. For some of us his art was what drew us in in the first place.

Without knowing it, I’ve been exposed to John’s art since my childhood, all the early edition 40k box arts are John’s pieces. The bright red of the Blood Angels on the Second edition box, to the iconic Black Templars art used on the box of the Third edition, his iconic depiction of The Emperor of Mankind, the iconic Sister of Battle stood on the hill with her foot on a skull, his incredible work on the Mordheim rulebook… the list just goes on and on.

John Blanche will forever live on through his art and the inspiration he has provided for others. A true legend.
entry.summary: A short tribute to painting legend John Blanche, who passed away this year.

entry.title: No Longer
entry.content:
I see myself through my own eyes.

But they are no longer my eyes

This is no longer my body

These are no longer my legs

These are no longer my arms

I need to scream

But this is no longer my mouth

The arms that are no longer mine move

They have moved like this before

They will move like this again

They will move like this forever

I see this with eyes that are no longer mine

I remember, with a memory that is no longer my own
